title: Milhouse Of Sand And Fog

jokes.text: Maggie using the Bible inappropriately (presumably as a teething toy or to scratch with)
jokes.setup: Family bowing heads in silent prayer at church
jokes.punchline: Baby Maggie misusing the holy book for practical purposes
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons baby-misuses-adult-world humor, well-integrated with church setting

jokes.text: What? I forgot to brush my teeth this morning. Something's wrong with Maggie. We need to take her to Dr. Hibbert.
jokes.setup: Marge notices something wrong with Maggie after church incident
jokes.punchline: The 'something wrong' is just forgetting to brush teeth, trivializing the Bible misuse
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke but fairly predictable resolution

jokes.text: The Hibberts frequent a more 'boisterous' place of worship.
jokes.setup: Can't find Dr. Hibbert at their quiet church
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to Black church being more energetic
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character voice, though treads cultural stereotypes carefully

jokes.text: Fantastic! The black God rocks!
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing energetic Black church service
jokes.punchline: Homer's typically blunt and inappropriate enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Very Homer-specific inappropriate enthusiasm, though culturally sensitive territory

jokes.text: Say Amen! / Amen! Put this Calamine lotion on Maggie and the spots will heal. / The spots will heal! / The spots will heal!
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert giving medical advice during church service
jokes.punchline: Congregation treating medical diagnosis like a religious call-and-response
jokes.explanation: Clever fusion of medical and religious contexts, good escalation

jokes.text: Heal my spots, Lord! Praise the Lord! I can walk again! / What? My foot fell asleep.
jokes.setup: Dramatic religious healing testimony
jokes.punchline: Mundane explanation deflates the miracle
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection with good setup/payoff timing

jokes.text: What? My foot fell asleep.
jokes.setup: Dramatic claim of miraculous walking recovery
jokes.punchline: Mundane medical explanation deflating the miracle
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of religious fervor with prosaic reality. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: No! No! Here, play with your teddy bear.
jokes.setup: Trying to stop Maggie from scratching chickenpox
jokes.punchline: Presumably Maggie immediately starts scratching the teddy bear instead
jokes.explanation: Standard baby comedy, nothing special but functional

jokes.text: Homer, don't touch her! You haven't had chickenpox. While you have, and you're perfect... I'm just saying it's dangerous to get it as an adult. You could become sterile... Down low.
jokes.setup: Medical warning about adult chickenpox
jokes.punchline: Marge gets uncomfortably specific about Homer's fertility
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Marge's characteristic oversharing

jokes.text: Do you always have to get crude like that, Marge? You're capable of so much better than that.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing Marge's crudeness
jokes.punchline: The irony of Homer, of all people, calling someone crude
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reversal - Homer being prudish is inherently funny

jokes.text: Don't worry, Dad, I'm saving one for you. I'll leave it here in the dog's mouth. / No! Bart, that's a very bad storage space!
jokes.setup: Bart 'helpfully' saving a beer ice cube for Homer
jokes.punchline: Using the dog's mouth as refrigeration, Homer's measured objection
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd child logic combined with Homer's understated response

jokes.text: What's the confusion? Did you drink some frozen tea with a strange taste?
jokes.setup: Homer confused about something
jokes.punchline: Moe's matter-of-fact suggestion about drinking dog-mouth beer
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Moe's characteristic directness

jokes.text: Seriously? You mind if I bring my boys over to get infected? Then we'll have chickenpox in both houses. / Why would you do that? Are you a bad parent?
jokes.setup: Ned asking to bring kids over to get infected
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt question about parenting quality
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Ned's logic and Homer's directness

jokes.text: And they pay handsomely for the privilege? / They could. But you'd have to be a real money-grubber to charge friends and neighbors... $15 for your kids to get infected! Now with the Pox-Box.
jokes.setup: Homer getting a business idea from chickenpox parties
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to commercial for exactly that product
jokes.explanation: Great satirical timing - Homer's greed immediately matched by capitalism

jokes.text: Hi. Luann will pick up Milhouse when the party's over. I'll be reading in the car until then.
jokes.setup: Kirk dropping off Milhouse at the party
jokes.punchline: Pathetically waiting in car instead of socializing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk characterization - sad and awkward divorced dad behavior

jokes.text: Kirk, come with the parents to the kitchen. We're drinking alcoholic beverages and burying the missing parents.
jokes.setup: Homer inviting Kirk to join other parents
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of 'burying' absent parents like gossip
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual cruelty about gossip, good dark edge

jokes.text: I'm not one to make fun of others' diuretic problems... but you should have seen... There he is.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he doesn't make fun of people's problems
jokes.punchline: Immediately preparing to do exactly that
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer hypocrisy setup with good self-awareness

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, I present the girl with the crusts, the kid with all the spots, Maggie!
jokes.setup: Homer presenting Maggie like a carnival act
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top showman introduction for a sick baby
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate showmanship perfectly in character

jokes.text: I already feel like a chicken. I just laid an egg in my pants.
jokes.setup: Ralph feeling sick from chickenpox
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent/disturbing non-sequitur about egg-laying
jokes.explanation: Peak Ralph Wiggum - innocent absurdity with disturbing undertones

jokes.text: Chief, don't you think you've had enough? / I'm fine. Look, I can touch my gun with my nose. / Don't worry, I'm chief police. I meant to say... / Why doesn't Lou love me anymore? Why?
jokes.setup: Lou expressing concern about Chief's drinking
jokes.punchline: Chief's deteriorating attempts to prove sobriety, ending in emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from professional to personal breakdown, very Wiggum

jokes.text: Knock knock. / Luann, what are you doing here? I thought you were just dropping off Milhouse. You could go for coffee and come back. / No, stay. I'll go home. Who will let me suck gasoline?
jokes.setup: Awkward divorced couple interaction
jokes.punchline: Kirk's pathetic plea about gasoline addiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk moment - pathetic dependency hidden as casual question

jokes.text: Perfect! I never drink those without cigarette butts in them.
jokes.setup: Marge offering Margaritas
jokes.punchline: Luann's disturbing preference for cigarette-contaminated drinks
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully gross and specific character detail, very quotable

jokes.text: Hi kids! This fat guy only paid for 20 seconds, so listen. Moses got chickenpox and scratched day and night. He asked the rabbi what to do, and he said... Time's up. I'm out.
jokes.setup: Krusty starting to tell a joke/story
jokes.punchline: Cutting off mid-punchline due to payment expiring
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about entertainment industry economics, great structure

jokes.text: Time's up. I'm outta here.
jokes.setup: Krusty starting to tell joke about Moses
jokes.punchline: Literally cutting off mid-joke when time expires
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-joke structure. Perfect economy - the abrupt cutoff IS the punchline. Meta-commentary on cheap entertainment.

jokes.text: Kirk, you look great. Do you work out? / Dogs chase me. / Nobody chases me lately.
jokes.setup: Marge complimenting Kirk's fitness
jokes.punchline: Kirk's pathetic reason (being chased) and Luann's sad comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect divorced couple sadness, excellent economy in the exchange

jokes.text: Everyone has hope now.
jokes.setup: Kirk asking if there's hope for him romantically
jokes.punchline: Luann's crushingly matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Devastating in its simplicity, perfect deadpan delivery implied

jokes.text: Sweet Lizzie McGuire! / Do you consider this traumatic?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's pop culture reference exclamation
jokes.punchline: Someone questioning whether this qualifies as traumatic
jokes.explanation: Typical Comic Book Guy pop culture reference with mild subversion

jokes.text: Mr. Stomach, are you hungry? / I'm always hungry. Finish the pizza, fatty.
jokes.setup: Homer talking to his stomach
jokes.punchline: His stomach talking back with harsh criticism
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer absurdism with self-deprecating twist

jokes.text: I have to do this for work.
jokes.setup: Homer eating compulsively
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's job-related rather than personal
jokes.explanation: Good Homer rationalization humor, typical denial

jokes.text: Mr. Van Houten, I like how you kiss me! / Yes, it's fantastic kissing women. / Baby, kiss my mouth. / Hug me like we're in a luxury hotel.
jokes.setup: Milhouse witnessing his parents being affectionate
jokes.punchline: Overly explicit and awkward romantic dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about inappropriate parental behavior

jokes.text: But how? / I have no idea. I stayed away from Maggie all day. It's itching incredibly!
jokes.setup: Homer somehow caught chickenpox despite avoiding Maggie
jokes.punchline: Homer scratching frantically while denying contact
jokes.explanation: Setup for Homer's chickenpox plot, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Son, we want to talk to you about what you saw at the Simpsons'. / I didn't see anything that gives me hope, if that's what you mean.
jokes.setup: Parents wanting to discuss what Milhouse witnessed
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's bleak interpretation deflating their hopes
jokes.explanation: Good setup subversion with Milhouse's characteristic pessimism

jokes.text: Maybe you should raise your hopes a little. / They're already high! They've reached the clouds! Your father and I are going through a trial 'un-divorce.'
jokes.setup: Luann suggesting Milhouse be more hopeful
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's over-the-top enthusiasm and the 'un-divorce' concept
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'un-divorce' and character-appropriate enthusiasm

jokes.text: I want to clarify something. Unlike divorce, this isn't your fault.
jokes.setup: Kirk trying to reassure Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Accidentally confirming the divorce WAS Milhouse's fault
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Kirk accidentally revealing terrible truth while trying to be kind

jokes.text: I can't believe it, my family might get back together.
jokes.setup: Kirk's accidental revelation about divorce being Milhouse's fault
jokes.punchline: Milhouse completely missing the implication and staying optimistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse obliviousness, good callback to previous dark joke

jokes.text: I haven't seen colorful hallucinations in a long time.
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing chickenpox fever
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to regular hallucinations as if normal
jokes.explanation: Great Homer line - casually revealing disturbing personal history

jokes.text: Oat-Homer. Stop eating the oat flakes. That's the third bathtub you've eaten.
jokes.setup: Homer in oatmeal bath for chickenpox relief
jokes.punchline: Homer eating the medicinal bath, multiple bathtubs worth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - can't resist eating even medicinal oatmeal, great escalation

jokes.text: Please? No, I won't come to work today. I have chickenpox. I know I said that last month too, but I was lying. Yes, okay. Yes. Okay. I love you, goodbye.
jokes.setup: Homer calling in sick to work
jokes.punchline: Admitting previous lies and inappropriate 'I love you' to boss
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's inappropriate workplace behavior

jokes.text: They just fired me. Let me look at the want ads. Truck drivers wanted in Iraq. Trucks are like big cars.
jokes.setup: Homer getting fired and job hunting
jokes.punchline: Considering dangerous Iraq job with naive understanding of trucks
jokes.explanation: Homer's dangerous naivety perfectly captured, good dark edge

jokes.text: Truck drivers wanted in Iraq. Trucks are like big cars.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at job listings
jokes.punchline: Oversimplified understanding of dangerous job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - reducing complex dangerous situation to simple terms. Dark humor about war zone work.

jokes.text: The captain and I... / I don't want to know.
jokes.setup: Kirk about to confess affair during separation
jokes.punchline: Luann cutting him off immediately
jokes.explanation: Good awkward couple dynamics, efficient comedy

jokes.text: Milly... / Botanical Garden? Bilderberg Club? Rugby field with the Springfield Stans?
jokes.setup: Milhouse being asked what the family should do
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and sophisticated activity suggestions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from normal to conspiracy theory activities, very Milhouse

jokes.text: Son, here's $10. Why don't you go to the bowling alley and throw some lonely balls? But tonight there's Champions League!
jokes.setup: Kirk trying to get rid of Milhouse for romantic time
jokes.punchline: 'Lonely balls' phrase and Milhouse's priorities
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and character priorities clash

jokes.text: This 'un-divorce' sucks. My parents used to compete for my love... and I always came out winning. And the plant got watered along with the royal treatment. Movies, double Christmas, soda with meals... We lived like kings.
jokes.setup: Milhouse complaining about parents getting back together
jokes.punchline: Missing the benefits of being a child of divorce
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical divorce trauma narrative, great specificity

jokes.text: Movies, double Christmas, soda with meals... We lived like kings.
jokes.setup: Milhouse describing benefits of parental competition
jokes.punchline: Treating basic luxuries as royal treatment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and child's perspective on what constitutes luxury. Character-appropriate values.

jokes.text: Unforgettable New Year's. / I think we need to break up your parents again.
jokes.setup: Milhouse reminiscing about divorce benefits
jokes.punchline: Bart's practical solution to break them up again
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Bart's characteristic scheming

jokes.text: You know, Bart, my imaginary friend... never makes me do things I think are wrong. That's why he had to leave forever. But I thought you'd let him come back. I'm afraid he'll spend another year searching his mind.
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining his imaginary friend's moral compass
jokes.punchline: The friend left because Milhouse does too many wrong things
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully twisted psychology - imaginary friend has higher standards than real person

jokes.text: But I thought you'd let him come back. I'm afraid he'll spend another year searching his mind.
jokes.setup: Continuing imaginary friend storyline
jokes.punchline: Treating imaginary friend's exile as real journey
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of absurd imaginary friend logic. Maintains internal consistency of bizarre premise.

jokes.text: Your mom said I smell and that I'm crude. / Mom said that? / That's amazing. Usually she kept criticism inside until it destroyed our marriage.
jokes.setup: Kirk learning Luann criticized him openly
jokes.punchline: Being grateful for honest criticism that could have saved marriage
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark relationship insight with perfect Kirk characterization

jokes.text: I don't want to rat out Dad, but he just let me drink a beer! / Looks like we have a problem here. And the problem is you're addicted to lies.
jokes.setup: Milhouse tattling on his father
jokes.punchline: Luann diagnosing Milhouse instead of addressing the issue
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - parent ignoring actual problem to focus on child's honesty

jokes.text: But I'm drunk! I'll prove it to you. Watch me... kiss this photo of Nana. I can't do it. She's so mean to me. She won't let me eat jellybeans!
jokes.setup: Milhouse trying to prove he's drunk
jokes.punchline: Unable to kiss grandma photo due to candy restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from trying to prove drunkenness to family grievances

jokes.text: Maybe my parents are made for each other, Bart. / You better hope they're not, because you know what comes next? Your worst nightmare: A little sister.
jokes.setup: Milhouse being optimistic about parents' reunion
jokes.punchline: Bart warning about sibling consequences
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later plot, Bart's characteristic cynicism

jokes.text: If you don't mind, I want to watch... that 'cool' series about teenagers in California.
jokes.setup: Milhouse wanting to watch TV
jokes.punchline: Awkward description of teen drama as 'cool' in quotes
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about Milhouse trying to seem sophisticated

jokes.text: I'm sure Sterling put that bra there to cause trouble. / Amazing idea! These screenwriters are geniuses! However much they're paid, it's not enough.
jokes.setup: Commenting on TV show plot device
jokes.punchline: Homer praising screenwriters while being a TV character himself
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about TV writing from TV characters

jokes.text: That bra will break up and separate. It will break up your mother's doubts and separate your parents.
jokes.setup: Bart getting scheming idea from TV
jokes.punchline: Convoluted plan involving planted evidence
jokes.explanation: Good Bart scheming moment with logical plan development

jokes.text: This isn't mine. / PROPERTY OF MARGE SIMPSON
jokes.setup: Kirk denying ownership of found bra
jokes.punchline: Clear labeling immediately contradicting his denial
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag with perfect timing and clear contradiction

jokes.text: I have a message for your wife. Stop sleeping with my husband! / And you are... / The wife of your wife's lover.
jokes.setup: Luann confronting Homer about affair
jokes.punchline: Confusing relationship description
jokes.explanation: Good awkward confrontation with relationship complexity humor

jokes.text: That's ridiculous! No woman wants sex with that loser. / And congratulations on taking him back. Good move.
jokes.setup: Homer defending Kirk's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Luann's sarcastic congratulations on reunion
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics and sarcasm

jokes.text: That's Marge's bra. I bought it for her boobs! / Of all the people Marge could cheat with, why did she choose me? I'm devastated.
jokes.setup: Homer's crude declaration of bra ownership
jokes.punchline: Kirk's self-deprecating devastation at being chosen
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Homer's crudeness and Kirk's low self-esteem

jokes.text: How can you ask me something like that? / I repeat: Did you sleep with Kirk? / Homer, you've lost it! / You're not saying 'no.' / Very well... No! / I still don't hear 'no.' / No! No! No!
jokes.setup: Homer's accusations about Marge cheating
jokes.punchline: Homer's impossible-to-satisfy demands for denial
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of marital conflict with Homer's unreasonable demands

jokes.text: We tried to break up your parents and broke up mine instead. / Want to talk to my psychiatrist about how you feel? / Yes, Dr. Wexler? Stop calling me, I'm on my honeymoon! / Yeah, it's that crazy kid again.
jokes.setup: Plan backfiring, seeking professional help
jokes.punchline: Psychiatrist on honeymoon, dismissing patient
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and professional boundary humor

jokes.text: I'd do anything to have your mom back and that donut. / Look, you and Mom can work things out. What exactly happened?
jokes.setup: Homer expressing what he misses most
jokes.punchline: Equally mourning wife and donut
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - wife and food equally important

jokes.text: Better go. / Where do you live? / You know the 'Four Seasons'? I'm experiencing them firsthand because I live in the park.
jokes.setup: Asking where Homer lives now
jokes.punchline: Clever wordplay about hotel name vs. actual seasons outdoors
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect setup/payoff structure, very quotable

jokes.text: I can read you like an open book. / You read books! If you don't tell Mom what you did, I will.
jokes.setup: Lisa using book metaphor
jokes.punchline: Bart's surprise that Lisa reads books
jokes.explanation: Good sibling banter with character-appropriate anti-intellectualism

jokes.text: You'll marry much older men as compensation, while I'll become one of those weirdos who's 35 and goes to high school basketball games.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining divorce consequences
jokes.punchline: Specific disturbing prediction about his future
jokes.explanation: Darkly specific and disturbing prediction. Good psychological insight into divorce effects presented humorously.

jokes.text: I looked into it and found out I was to blame. / Really? Just for that, I'll delete all the saved games on your PlayStation. / What? How do you know such tricks? / I read it right here.
jokes.setup: Bart confessing and expecting lenient punishment
jokes.punchline: Marge's sophisticated and cruel punishment method
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Marge being unexpectedly tech-savvy and cruel

jokes.text: That means you and Dad will get D-I-V-O-R-C-E? / Young man, go study spelling... or I'll delete all the sounds from your phone. / No!
jokes.setup: Bart spelling out divorce
jokes.punchline: Marge's continued tech-savvy threats
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Marge's unexpected technical knowledge

jokes.text: How does this look, Bart? A traditional dance could solve our problems. All I need is your pants size. It's two sizes bigger than your pants. How many times must I tell you? Forget Rodgers and Hammerstein! Rodgers and Hart? No!
jokes.setup: Milhouse suggesting musical theater solution
jokes.punchline: Getting increasingly frustrated about musical theater references
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's over-the-top theatrical solutions with specific Broadway knowledge

jokes.text: The stupid TV show plots don't work in real life. Mom and Dad's marriage is solid as a rock... and they need to find the solution through analysis so it doesn't crumble.
jokes.setup: Lisa rejecting TV-inspired solutions
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally giving Bart idea with 'rock' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary and setup for Bart's final scheme

jokes.text: When my parents see this doll... falling off the cliff, they'll think it's me... and realize the most important thing is staying united. Do you like the doll? I even made it smell like you.
jokes.setup: Bart's elaborate fake-death scheme
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of making doll smell like Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Bart's attention to disturbing details

jokes.text: Questions? / Yes, what will you dress as for Halloween? / Giant dog poop. Let's begin.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for questions about the plan
jokes.punchline: Milhouse asking about completely unrelated Halloween costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - completely missing the point with innocent question

jokes.text: I can't tell who's who. / I already started running!
jokes.setup: Plan going wrong with identity confusion
jokes.punchline: Bart already in motion despite confusion
jokes.explanation: Action sequence humor, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Homer, I can save you both. Just let go of the rock! Trust me!
jokes.setup: Dramatic rescue scene
jokes.punchline: Asking someone to let go during a cliff rescue
jokes.explanation: Standard rescue scene humor, not particularly innovative

jokes.text: Mom, Dad, I'd give 3 trillion to fix things between you. / Make it 2 trillion. We'll lose the first trillion in taxes!
jokes.setup: Bart's emotional offer of money
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately negotiating and worrying about taxes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - ruining emotional moment with practical concerns, great character integration

jokes.text: Can he swim? / What do you think?
jokes.setup: Concern about someone who fell in water
jokes.punchline: Implied knowledge that they obviously can't swim
jokes.explanation: Simple understated ending humor, economical but not remarkable

tmdb_overview: Never one to miss the opportunity to make a quick buck, Homer cashes in on Maggie's bout of chicken pox. He decides to host a Pox Party and charges parents to infect their children.
